question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an unprivileged maker, after lenders have taken buy offers and hold credit in a market, call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) to de-ratify the root, permanently blocking any future take of sell offers that would create withdrawable assets for those lenders, under the precondition that the market has no alternative liquidity source and all pending sell offers share the same root? Call sequence: maker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true); takers fill buy offers creating lender credit; maker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false); lenders attempt take of sell offers; isRatified reverts NotRatified. Invariant tested: lender credit must remain redeemable via some protocol path. Scoped impact: permanent freeze of lender credit if no withdrawable assets exist and all exit paths require the de-ratified root. Proof idea: fuzz root values and assert that after de-ratification no take succeeds for any offer in the tree, and lender creditOf remains nonzero with withdrawable == 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / isAuthorized] Can an authorized operator (isAuthorized[maker][operator] == true) call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) on behalf of the maker, freezing all offers in that Merkle tree, under the precondition that the maker has granted coarse-grained authorization to the operator for unrelated purposes (e.g., to manage collateral), and the operator is malicious or compromised? Call sequence: maker calls midnight.setIsAuthorized(operator, true, maker); operator calls setterRatifier.setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false); all takes for offers under root revert NotRatified. Invariant tested: authorization scope must not allow unintended ratifier state mutation. Scoped impact: all offers in the Merkle tree are frozen without maker's intent. Proof idea: unit test asserting that setIsRootRatified authorization check passes for any isAuthorized[maker][msg.sender] == true address, and fuzz operator addresses.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator A, who is authorized by maker, call midnight.setIsAuthorized(operatorB, true, maker) to grant operatorB full maker authorization, and then operatorB calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false), freezing the maker's offer tree, under the precondition that the authorization is coarse-grained and not scoped by action or contract? Call sequence: maker authorizes operatorA; operatorA calls midnight.setIsAuthorized(operatorB, true, maker); operatorB calls setterRatifier.setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false); all takes revert. Invariant tested: authorization delegation chain must not allow unauthorized third parties to mutate ratifier state. Scoped impact: transitive authorization abuse freezes all offers under root. Proof idea: state test asserting that isAuthorized[maker][operatorB] == true after two-hop delegation, and setIsRootRatified succeeds for operatorB.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can a taker be permanently frozen from taking a sell offer (which would repay their debt via credit netting) if the maker de-ratifies the root between the taker's mempool submission and on-chain execution, under the precondition that the taker has outstanding debt and the only available buy offer uses SetterRatifier with the now-de-ratified root? Call sequence: taker submits take tx; maker front-runs with setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false); taker's take reverts NotRatified; taker cannot reduce debt. Invariant tested: borrowers must always have a path to repay debt. Scoped impact: borrower debt cannot be reduced, collateral cannot be withdrawn, position becomes liquidatable. Proof idea: fork test simulating front-run ordering, assert debtOf(id, taker) unchanged after failed take.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / isRatified] Can a maker ratify a root, allow partial fills of offers in the tree, then de-ratify the root to freeze remaining capacity, under the precondition that consumed[maker][group] < maxUnits and the remaining offer capacity is the only source of liquidity for borrowers needing to exit? Call sequence: maker ratifies root; takers partially fill offers; maker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false); remaining takers cannot fill; borrowers with debt cannot exit via credit netting. Invariant tested: partial fill must not leave remaining capacity permanently inaccessible. Scoped impact: long-term freeze of borrower exit path for remaining offer capacity. Proof idea: fuzz units filled and assert that after de-ratification, consumed[maker][group] < maxUnits but no further take succeeds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified] Can a non-Midnight caller directly invoke isRatified on SetterRatifier to probe or manipulate ratifier state, under the precondition that the caller is any address other than MIDNIGHT, and does the NotMidnight revert correctly prevent any state change or bypass? Call sequence: attacker calls setterRatifier.isRatified(offer, ratifierData) directly; expect NotMidnight revert. Invariant tested: isRatified must only be callable by MIDNIGHT. Scoped impact: if bypass exists, attacker could spoof ratification and take unauthorized offers. Proof idea: unit test asserting isRatified reverts for all msg.sender != MIDNIGHT, fuzz caller addresses.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can a market creator deploy SetterRatifier with a wrong or zero MIDNIGHT address, causing all isRatified calls to revert with NotMidnight, permanently freezing all takes in markets that use this ratifier, under the precondition that the constructor does not validate the MIDNIGHT address? Call sequence: attacker deploys SetterRatifier(address(0)); creates market with offer.ratifier = address(maliciousSetterRatifier); lenders publish offers; takers call take; isRatified reverts NotMidnight. Invariant tested: ratifier deployment must not silently create a permanently broken ratifier. Scoped impact: all offers in markets using the broken ratifier are permanently frozen. Proof idea: unit test deploying SetterRatifier with address(0) and asserting all isRatified calls revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified / HashLib.isLeaf] Can a taker supply a ratifierData with leafIndex = 0 and an empty proof array when the root equals HashLib.hashOffer(offer), bypassing the Merkle proof check, under the precondition that the maker has ratified a root that happens to equal the offer hash? Call sequence: maker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, HashLib.hashOffer(offer), true); taker calls take with ratifierData = abi.encode(HashLib.hashOffer(offer), 0, new bytes32[](0)); isLeaf returns true since currentHash == root with empty proof. Invariant tested: single-leaf tree ratification must only authorize the exact offer hashed. Scoped impact: if root == offerHash is ratified, any offer with that hash can be taken without a proof. Proof idea: unit test asserting isLeaf(root, root, 0, []) == true and that this is the intended behavior.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified / HashLib.isLeaf] Can a taker supply a leafIndex that is out of range for the given proof length, causing HashLib.isLeaf to revert with LeafIndexOutOfRange, permanently blocking takes for valid offers when the taker or router supplies an incorrect leafIndex, under the precondition that the ratifierData is constructed off-chain and the leafIndex is miscalculated? Call sequence: maker ratifies root; taker calls take with leafIndex >= 2**proof.length; isLeaf reverts LeafIndexOutOfRange; take reverts. Invariant tested: valid offers in a ratified tree must always be takeable with correct proof. Scoped impact: off-chain routing errors cause permanent take failure for valid offers. Proof idea: fuzz leafIndex and proof.length, assert revert when leafIndex >> proof.length != 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / isRatified] Can a maker ratify a root containing a Merkle tree of offers across multiple markets, then de-ratify the root to simultaneously freeze all offers in all those markets, under the precondition that the same root is used for offers in different markets and the maker de-ratifies after positions are open? Call sequence: maker builds Merkle tree with offers for marketA and marketB; ratifies root; takers open positions in both markets; maker de-ratifies root; all remaining takes in both markets revert. Invariant tested: de-ratification of a shared root must not freeze unrelated markets. Scoped impact: cross-market freeze of all offers sharing the same root. Proof idea: state test with two markets sharing a root, assert both markets' take paths fail after de-ratification.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can a maker ratify a root, allow takers to open debt positions, then de-ratify the root to prevent liquidators from using take-based liquidation paths (post-maturity unwinding via sell offers), under the precondition that the market is post-maturity and liquidation incentive ramps from 1 to LIFmax over 15 minutes? Call sequence: maker ratifies root; borrowers open debt; market reaches maturity; maker de-ratifies root; liquidators cannot take sell offers to unwind; borrowers' overdue debt cannot be liquidated via take path. Invariant tested: overdue debt after maturity must remain liquidatable. Scoped impact: post-maturity liquidation freeze if take-based unwinding is the only path. Proof idea: warp to maturity+1, assert liquidate() still works independently of ratifier state.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified] Can a taker supply malformed ratifierData (e.g., truncated ABI encoding) causing abi.decode to revert inside isRatified, which propagates as a revert through take, under the precondition that the taker or router constructs ratifierData incorrectly? Call sequence: taker calls take with ratifierData = abi.encode(bytes32(0)) (missing leafIndex and proof); abi.decode reverts; take reverts. Invariant tested: malformed ratifierData must not permanently freeze valid offers. Scoped impact: routing layer bugs cause take failures for valid ratified offers. Proof idea: fuzz ratifierData byte length and assert revert for all lengths < 96 bytes (minimum for root + leafIndex + empty proof array).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / midnight.setIsAuthorized] Can a maker revoke SetterRatifier's authorization in Midnight (isAuthorized[maker][setterRatifier] = false) after ratifying a root, causing all subsequent takes to revert with RatifierUnauthorized at the take() level before isRatified is even called, under the precondition that the maker revokes authorization while offers are still active? Call sequence: maker authorizes setterRatifier; maker ratifies root; takers begin taking; maker calls midnight.setIsAuthorized(setterRatifier, false, maker); subsequent takes revert RatifierUnauthorized. Invariant tested: ratifier authorization revocation must not freeze existing positions. Scoped impact: all remaining offer capacity is frozen; lenders with credit cannot exit via sell offers. Proof idea: unit test asserting take reverts RatifierUnauthorized when isAuthorized[maker][ratifier] == false, independent of isRootRatified state.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Unlike EcrecoverRatifier.cancelRoot which is one-way (irreversible), SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified is two-way (reversible). Can a griefing attacker who is authorized by the maker repeatedly toggle isRootRatified between true and false to cause a DoS on takes, under the precondition that the attacker has isAuthorized[maker][attacker] == true and can front-run take transactions? Call sequence: attacker monitors mempool; sees take tx; front-runs with setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false); take reverts; attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) to restore. Invariant tested: ratifier state must not be cheaply togglable to DoS takes. Scoped impact: long-term DoS of take path freezing lender/borrower exit. Proof idea: gas cost analysis of toggle vs take, assert attacker can profitably DoS.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / multicall] Can an attacker use midnight.multicall to atomically de-ratify a root and execute a take in the same transaction, under the precondition that multicall uses delegatecall and the attacker controls both the ratifier state and the take parameters? Call sequence: attacker calls midnight.multicall([setIsRootRatified_calldata, take_calldata]); note setIsRootRatified is on SetterRatifier not Midnight, so delegatecall won't reach it. Invariant tested: multicall must not allow atomic ratifier state manipulation combined with take. Scoped impact: if reachable, attacker could take offers while simultaneously invalidating them for others. Proof idea: verify multicall cannot reach SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified via delegatecall.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified / HashLib.hashOffer] Can two structurally different Offer structs produce the same HashLib.hashOffer output (collision), allowing a taker to use a ratified root for an unintended offer, under the precondition that the hash function has a collision in the ABI-encoded offer fields? Call sequence: attacker finds offer A and offer B with hashOffer(A) == hashOffer(B); maker ratifies root containing A; attacker takes B using proof for A. Invariant tested: offer hash must uniquely identify the offer. Scoped impact: unauthorized take of unintended offer terms. Proof idea: differential fuzz test asserting hashOffer(A) != hashOffer(B) for all A != B.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified / HashLib.isLeaf] Can a taker supply a valid proof for offer A at leafIndex 0 but pass offer B (with the same hash as A) to isRatified, causing the Merkle proof to verify for the wrong offer, under the precondition that hashOffer(A) == hashOffer(B) due to ABI encoding ambiguity in dynamic fields like callbackData? Call sequence: maker ratifies root with offer A; attacker constructs offer B with same hash; attacker calls take(offerB, ratifierData_for_A); isLeaf passes; isRatified returns CALLBACK_SUCCESS. Invariant tested: Merkle proof must bind to the exact offer struct passed to take. Scoped impact: unauthorized take under wrong offer terms. Proof idea: fuzz callbackData and other dynamic fields to find hash collisions.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can a market creator create a market with a SetterRatifier as the ratifier field in offers, then never call setIsRootRatified, causing all takes to permanently revert with NotRatified, freezing any lender who published offers expecting the ratifier to be activated? Call sequence: market created; lender publishes offer with ratifier = setterRatifier; isRootRatified[maker][root] == false (default); taker calls take; isRatified reverts NotRatified. Invariant tested: default ratifier state must not permanently block valid offers. Scoped impact: lender offers are permanently unfillable if maker never ratifies. Proof idea: unit test asserting take reverts when isRootRatified is never set to true.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / isRatified] Can a maker ratify root R1 containing offer O1, then de-ratify R1 and ratify root R2 containing the same offer O1 (with a different tree structure), causing takers who cached the old proof for R1 to permanently fail, under the precondition that the taker's ratifierData references R1 which is no longer ratified? Call sequence: maker ratifies R1; taker caches proof for O1 under R1; maker de-ratifies R1, ratifies R2; taker submits take with old ratifierData (R1, leafIndex, proof); isRatified reverts NotRatified. Invariant tested: root rotation must not permanently freeze takers with stale proofs. Scoped impact: takers with cached proofs are frozen until they obtain new proofs for R2. Proof idea: state test simulating root rotation and asserting old ratifierData fails.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can a maker ratify a root for a buy offer (maker is lender), allow borrowers to take and accumulate debt, then de-ratify the root to prevent any further takes that would allow borrowers to exit debt via credit netting (buying units to reduce debt), under the precondition that the market has no other buy offers available? Call sequence: maker (lender) ratifies root with buy offer; borrowers take, accumulating debt; maker de-ratifies root; borrowers cannot take buy offers to reduce debt; borrowers must repay directly or be liquidated. Invariant tested: borrowers must always have a path to reduce debt. Scoped impact: borrowers cannot exit debt via credit netting; forced to repay full amount or face liquidation. Proof idea: assert debtOf(id, borrower) > 0 and no take path available after de-ratification.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / claimContinuousFee] Can a maker de-ratify a root to prevent takes that would increase withdrawable assets, indirectly freezing the feeClaimer's ability to claim continuous fees (since claimContinuousFee requires withdrawable >= amount), under the precondition that continuousFeeCredit > 0 but withdrawable == 0 because no repayments or liquidations have occurred? Call sequence: maker ratifies root; takes occur, credit accumulates; maker de-ratifies root; no further takes create withdrawable; feeClaimer calls claimContinuousFee; reverts if withdrawable < amount. Invariant tested: fee claim paths must not be blocked by ratifier state. Scoped impact: continuous fee claims are frozen if withdrawable is zero and ratifier blocks new takes. Proof idea: assert claimContinuousFee reverts when withdrawable == 0 and no repayments exist.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified] Can a reentrancy attack occur during isRatified (which is view) if the SetterRatifier's isRootRatified mapping is read while a callback is in progress that modifies the mapping, under the precondition that a callback contract calls setIsRootRatified during a take's callback execution? Call sequence: take begins; buyerCallback is called; callback calls setIsRootRatifier.setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false); isRatified was already called before callback so no effect on current take; but subsequent takes in same tx via multicall would fail. Invariant tested: ratifier state changes during callbacks must not corrupt in-flight take state. Scoped impact: callback-triggered de-ratification freezes subsequent takes in multicall. Proof idea: multicall test with callback that de-ratifies root, assert second take in multicall fails.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can a maker ratify a root containing a sell offer with reduceOnly = true, allow lenders to take (reducing their credit), then de-ratify the root to prevent lenders from further reducing credit via the reduceOnly offer, under the precondition that the lender's only exit path is through this reduceOnly sell offer? Call sequence: maker ratifies root with reduceOnly sell offer; lenders take, reducing credit; maker de-ratifies root; lenders cannot further reduce credit; lenders are stuck with remaining credit. Invariant tested: reduceOnly offers must not be the sole exit path that can be frozen. Scoped impact: lender credit is frozen if reduceOnly offer is the only exit. Proof idea: assert creditOf(id, lender) > 0 and no take path available after de-ratification of reduceOnly offer.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / isRatified] Can a taker supply a proof with proof.length > 20, causing HashLib.offerTreeTypeHash to revert with TreeTooHigh (in EcrecoverRatifier), but in SetterRatifier which does not call offerTreeTypeHash, does isLeaf still process arbitrarily long proofs, and can this be used to construct a valid proof for an unratified root? Call sequence: taker constructs proof with length 21; SetterRatifier.isRatified calls HashLib.isLeaf (no TreeTooHigh check); isLeaf processes proof; if root matches, isRatified passes. Invariant tested: proof length must be bounded consistently between ratifier implementations. Scoped impact: if SetterRatifier accepts proofs of arbitrary length, it may accept proofs for roots not intended by the maker. Proof idea: unit test with proof.length = 21 asserting SetterRatifier.isRatified does not revert TreeTooHigh.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can a maker ratify a root for a market that has not yet been created (touchMarket not yet called), and then the market is created with different parameters than expected, causing the ratified root to correspond to offers for a different market ID, under the precondition that market creation is permissionless and the root is computed before market creation? Call sequence: maker computes root for expected market params; maker ratifies root; market is created with different params (different maturity, different collateral); root corresponds to wrong market; takers cannot use ratified root for the actual market. Invariant tested: ratified roots must correspond to the correct market. Scoped impact: ratified root is useless for the actual market, freezing all offers. Proof idea: assert hashOffer includes all market params and that root mismatch causes InvalidProof.
